heading: Transportation of explosive, biological, chemical, or radioactive or nuclear materials
label: 2283 Transportation of explosive, biological, chemical, or radioactive or nuclear materials
notes: References in TextSection 831(f)(1), referred to in subsec. (c)(5), was redesignated section 831(g)(1) by , , .Pub. L. 114–23, title VIII, § 812(d)129 Stat. 312June 2, 2015
amendments: References in TextSection 831(f)(1), referred to in subsec. (c)(5), was redesignated section 831(g)(1) by , , .Pub. L. 114–23, title VIII, § 812(d)129 Stat. 312June 2, 2015

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: Whoever knowingly transports aboard any vessel within the United States and on waters subject to the jurisdiction of the United States or any vessel outside the United States and on the high seas or having United States nationality an explosive or incendiary device, biological agent, chemical weapon, or radioactive or nuclear material, knowing that any such item is intended to be used to commit an offense listed under section 2332b(g)(5)(B), shall be fined under this title or imprisoned for any term of years or for life, or both.

children.heading: .—Causing Death
children.label: b .—Causing Death
children.text: Any person who causes the death of a person by engaging in conduct prohibited by subsection (a) may be punished by death.

children.heading: .—Biological agent
children.label: 1 .—Biological agent
children.text: The term “biological agent” means any biological agent, toxin, or vector (as those terms are defined in section 178).

children.heading: .—By-product material
children.label: 2 .—By-product material
children.text: The term “by-product material” has the meaning given that term in section 11(e) of the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 ().42 U.S.C. 2014(e)

children.heading: .—Chemical weapon
children.label: 3 .—Chemical weapon
children.text: The term “chemical weapon” has the meaning given that term in section 229F(1).

children.heading: .—Explosive or incendiary device
children.label: 4 .—Explosive or incendiary device
children.text: The term “explosive or incendiary device” has the meaning given the term in section 232(5) and includes explosive materials, as that term is defined in section 841(c) and explosive as defined in section 844(j).

children.heading: .—Nuclear material
children.label: 5 .—Nuclear material
children.text: The term “nuclear material” has the meaning given that term in section 831(f)(1).1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: A
children.text: source material and special nuclear material, but does not include natural or depleted uranium;

children.label: C
children.text: material made radioactive by bombardment in an accelerator; or

children.heading: .—2So in original. No par. (7) has been enacted.2Source material
children.label: 8 .—2So in original. No par. (7) has been enacted.2Source material
children.text: The term “source material” has the meaning given that term in section 11(z) of the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 ().42 U.S.C. 2014(z)

children.heading: .—Special nuclear material
children.label: 9 .—Special nuclear material
children.text: The term “special nuclear material” has the meaning given that term in section 11(aa) of the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 ().42 U.S.C. 2014(aa)
